It belongs to the class Holothuroidea, which is a group of marine animals characterized by their elongated bodies and ability to move using their many tiny tube feet. The class Holothuroidea is further divided into several orders, with Leioligo erecta being part of the order Elasopogonida. This order is then grouped into the subclass Aspidochirotida, which is characterized by the presence of a calcareous ring and a distinctive pattern of skin papillae. The classification of Leioligo erecta reflects its unique characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other sea cucumber species.
